summary: The case presents an evenings-specific pattern: recurring heavy/bleak mood, minimal outward distress, and persistent passive smartphone scrolling driven by perceived high effort to engage in alternative activities and avoidance. Modifiable levers span the bio-psychological-social spectrum: stabilize sleep and energy (bio), reduce avoidance and increase activation (psycho), implement social connections and environmental supports (social), and employ measurement-based feedback to tailor the plan. The proposed solutions emphasize brief, scalable, low-friction changes that target evening behaviors, energy, and mood, with clear metrics and iterative monitoring over 2–6 weeks.

solutions.predictor_variable: Evening Behavioral Activation Scheduling (3x 10–15 min activities)
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured evening plan with 3 brief, enjoyable activities (e.g., 15-min walk, 10-min puzzle, 10-min call) initiated via a written/simple plan, with explicit daily completion tracking and weekly adjustments to ensure at least 3 activation moments per week.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Provides low-friction access to rewarding experiences, counteracts inertia, reduces reliance on passive scrolling, and lowers perceived effort by buffering with short, manageable tasks.
solutions.measurement: Evenings with at least one planned activity completed; number of activation tasks completed per week; self-rated evening energy before/after activity
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for mood worsening or overwhelm; if nocturnal mood shifts or self-harm risk emerges, escalate to a clinician and activate safety plan.
solutions.mapping_query: Link to CASE_PAYLOAD: reduce evening bleak mood via proactive activation; target C01/C03/C04/C05 with a 3-activation/week plan.

solutions.predictor_variable: Evening phone-use boundary and replacement cue
solutions.solution_candidate: Establish a 7–9 pm phone-free window; place devices out of reach; pre-plan alternative low-attack activities (e.g., journaling, light stretching); use reminders nudges to maintain boundary and track violations.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces automatic, prolonged scrolling that reinforces avoidance and drains executive energy, creating cognitive space for engaging in non-phone activities and reducing perceived effort to start.
solutions.measurement: Evening screen time (minutes) before vs during the boundary window; number of boundary violations; self-reported urge to scroll
solutions.safety_notes: If device-boundary causes distress or withdrawal, provide alternative coping options and ensure social contact remains available.
solutions.mapping_query: MAPS to C03/C05 by reducing avoidance-driven scrolling and facilitating engagement in alternative activities.

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep circadian alignment and light exposure in evenings
solutions.solution_candidate: Stabilize sleep with fixed bed (e.g., 10:30 pm) and wake times (7:30 am); dim evening lighting; reduce blue light; maximize morning light exposure; limit caffeine after 2 pm.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Circadian stabilization improves mood regulation and daytime energy, lowering evening heaviness and facilitating initiation of activities.
solutions.measurement: Bedtime/wake time logs; minutes of morning light exposure; evening energy/mood ratings
solutions.safety_notes: If sleep problems persist or worsen (insomnia), seek sleep assessment.
solutions.mapping_query: Biological rhythm optimization to attenuate C01 and support C04 via energy/mood improvement.

solutions.solution_candidate: 5-minute daily mindfulness or ACT exercise in the evening (breath, defusion, values-aligned action); brief cognitive defusion to reduce avoidance, commit to one small action post-practice.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces experiential avoidance and distress amplification, increasing willingness to engage in non-phone activities despite bleak mood.
solutions.measurement: Daily practice minutes; distress tolerance rating; number of evenings with non-phone activity after practice
solutions.safety_notes: No special safety concerns; monitor for anxiety escalation.
solutions.mapping_query: Relates to C01/C04/C05 by improving tolerance and reducing avoidance in evenings.

solutions.predictor_variable: Evening social connection and belonging activities
solutions.solution_candidate: Schedule 2 short (10–15 min) social interactions per week in the evening (call a friend, online chat, or brief in-person) to counter isolation and bleak affect.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social connectedness enhances mood and reduces rumination, increasing energy and reducing reliance on phone-based avoidance behaviors.
solutions.measurement: Number of evening social interactions; self-reported mood before/after interactions; belonging/connectedness ratings
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure activities are supportive; adjust if social conflict arises.
solutions.mapping_query: SOCIAL action to improve mood and reduce avoidance, addressing C01/C02/C04/C05.

solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a brief daily diary (2 minutes) capturing mood, energy, evening activities, and phone use; weekly review with simple plan adjustments; optional automated summaries.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Measurement-based feedback increases self-awareness, guides tuning of activation strategies, and maintains motivation for behavior change.
solutions.measurement: Diary completion rate; correlation between mood/energy and activities; weekly plan adjustment log
solutions.safety_notes: Maintain privacy of entries; no sensitive data exposure.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP to C01/C03/C04/C05 via data-informed adjustments.

solutions.solution_candidate: Provide a light, balanced evening snack and adequate hydration to sustain energy; limit high-sugar items late evening; maintain hydration; avoid late caffeine; align with energy needs to facilitate activity.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Stable energy availability supports motivation to initiate activities and reduces perceived barrier.
solutions.measurement: Evening snack logs; hydration intake; self-rated energy in the evening
solutions.mapping_query: BIO-level energy optimization to support C04 by reducing fatigue and barriers to action.
